description: Allow unauthenticated users to add reactions to comments. This requires removing the auth guard from the reaction endpoints on the backend and updating the frontend to show the React button even when not logged in.

tasks.task: In src/comments/comments.controller.ts, remove the @UseGuards(AuthGuard) decorator from the addReaction method (POST /comments/:commentId/reactions) and the removeReaction method (DELETE /comments/:commentId/reactions). The reaction endpoints should be publicly accessible. You may need to adjust the addReaction method to handle cases where there is no authenticated user - consider making reactions anonymous or using a session/IP-based identifier instead of userId.

In src/components/CommentSection.tsx, update the rendering logic to show the React button for ALL users, not just authenticated ones. Currently the React button is likely wrapped in a condition like {user && ...}. Remove this condition so unauthenticated users can see and click the React button. Also ensure the handleReaction function works without requiring a user token (if the backend accepts anonymous reactions).

example_frontend: Verify unauthenticated users CAN see the React button on comments
Verify unauthenticated users CAN add reactions to comments
Verify unauthenticated users CAN remove their reactions from comments
Verify authenticated users can still add reactions normally
Verify the reaction count updates correctly for both authenticated and unauthenticated users
